entityName: Rascal Does Not Dream of Bunny Girl Senpai

metadata.entityName: Rascal Does Not Dream of Bunny Girl Senpai

ingredients.name: Confort Fujisawa C-2
ingredients.entityContext: This apartment building in Fujisawa's Fujigaoka neighborhood is believed by pilgrimage researchers to be the model for the apartment building where Sakuta lives in the anime, making it a key residential location in the story.

ingredients.name: Fujisawa Police Station Fujisawaekikitaguchi Police Box
ingredients.localName: 藤沢警察署 藤沢駅北口交番

ingredients.name: Goshogatani Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: Goshogatani Bridge near Fujigaoka in Fujisawa is identified in anime pilgrimage guides as a location featured in walking scenes throughout the series, situated in the same residential neighborhood as other confirmed locations.

ingredients.name: Shichirigahama
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒248-0026 Kanagawa, Kamakura, Shichirigahamahigashi, 2-chōme−1, 七里ガ浜
ingredients.entityContext: Shichirigahama beach serves as the main coastal setting for Bunny Girl Senpai, where Sakuta and Mai share pivotal scenes against the backdrop of sweeping ocean views and the distant silhouette of Enoshima. The wide, sandy stretch is immediately recognizable as the show's signature outdoor location.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Sea Candle
ingredients.localName: 江の島シーキャンドル
ingredients.entityContext: The Enoshima Sea Candle observation tower appears prominently in the anime as a landmark backdrop and meeting point, its distinctive lighthouse silhouette visible throughout many seaside scenes in the Enoshima arc involving Futaba Rio and the beach episodes.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Benten Bridge
ingredients.address: Enoshima Benten Bridge, Enoshima, Fujisawa, Kanagawa 251-0036, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The long causeway bridge connecting Fujisawa to Enoshima island is featured in multiple walking scenes, most memorably when Sakuta traverses it during his efforts to understand the supernatural phenomenon affecting Mai and other characters.

ingredients.name: Near the Kuramae 34
ingredients.type: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店
categories: コーヒーショップ・喫茶店

ingredients.name: Taisho Bridge
ingredients.description: Taisho Bridge (大正橋) — local bridge in Kamakura, near Daibutsu Kiridoshi Pass and Daikamei Tunnel. Not listed on Google Maps, but the coordinates fall in this area — a verified scene location. Navigate by coordinates / Street View.
ingredients.entityContext: Taisho Bridge is identified by anime pilgrimage researchers as a scene location used in the series, appearing in shots of Sakuta walking through the streets of Fujisawa near the station district.

ingredients.name: @Park Fujisawa Asahicho
ingredients.localName: アットパーク藤沢朝日町

ingredients.name: Seven-Eleven Fujisawa Station North Exit
ingredients.localName: セブン-イレブン 藤沢駅北口店
ingredients.type: コンビニエンスストア
categories: コンビニエンスストア

ingredients.name: Yukiai Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: Yukiai Bridge in Kamakura appears in the anime as one of the local landmarks Sakuta passes through during his travels around the Kamakura/Shichirigahama area, with its stone balustrade captured in atmospheric pilgrimage-worthy shots.

ingredients.name: Katase Higashihama Beach
ingredients.entityContext: Katase East Beach near Enoshima is one of the beaches depicted in the anime's many coastal walking and conversation scenes between Sakuta and Mai, set against the sweeping Shonan coastline.

ingredients.name: Denny's Fujisawa North Exit
ingredients.localName: デニーズ 藤沢北口店
ingredients.type: ファミリー レストラン
categories: ファミリー レストラン
ingredients.entityContext: The Denny's family restaurant near Fujisawa Station's north exit appears as a background location in the anime's everyday Fujisawa scenes, consistent with the show's grounded depiction of suburban life around the station.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Glass HOOK Benzaiten Nakamise Street Store
ingredients.localName: 江の島ガラスHOOK 弁財天仲見世通り店

ingredients.name: Coin lockers outside the ticket gates at JR Fujisawa Station
ingredients.localName: JR藤沢駅 改札外コインロッカー

ingredients.name: Near the Union Monthly Fujisawa 2
ingredients.localName: ユニオンマンスリー藤沢２付近
ingredients.type: マンスリー マンション ビル
categories: マンスリー マンション ビル
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒251-0002 Kanagawa, Fujisawa, Daigiri, 1 Chome−4−28 興和ガーデンヒルズ

ingredients.name: Ema Rack
ingredients.entityContext: The ema wishing-board rack at Enoshima Shrine appears in scenes at the shrine, where visitors and characters engage with the traditional custom of writing wishes, a quiet and atmospheric moment in the Enoshima arc.

ingredients.name: Kamakurakoko-Mae Station
ingredients.entityContext: This small seaside Enoden station sits directly beside the ocean and iconic level crossing, featured in key atmospheric shots throughout the anime and widely recognized by fans as one of the show's most evocative real-world locations.

ingredients.name: Fujisawa Station North Exit Grass Square
ingredients.localName: 藤沢駅北口 芝生広場
ingredients.entityContext: The grassy plaza at Fujisawa Station's north exit is seen in the anime as a transitional space where characters depart and arrive, establishing Fujisawa as the central hub for the story's everyday life scenes.

ingredients.name: Enoshima Shrine - Hetsumiya
ingredients.entityContext: The main shrine on Enoshima island appears in the anime as a spiritual and atmospheric backdrop, with its stone torii gates and traditional Shinto architecture featured during scenes on the island.

ingredients.name: Fujisawa City Main Library
ingredients.localName: 藤沢市総合市民図書館
ingredients.entityContext: The Fujisawa City Main Library is featured in the anime as a location Sakuta visits to research the supernatural 'Adolescence Syndrome' phenomena, with its reading rooms providing a quiet academic atmosphere.

ingredients.name: Observatory Square Wood Deck
ingredients.localName: 展望台広場ウッドデッキ
ingredients.entityContext: The wooden deck observation platform on Enoshima island provides the sweeping Pacific ocean views seen in scenic shots during the anime's Enoshima episodes, with Mount Fuji visible on clear days just as depicted.

ingredients.name: Karaoke Best 10 Fujisawa
ingredients.localName: カラオケベスト10 藤沢店

ingredients.name: Ueno Zoological Gardens
ingredients.entityContext: Ueno Zoo in Tokyo appears in the anime during a key date scene between Sakuta and Mai, a memorable moment where the two spend time together amid the animals, deepening their relationship.

ingredients.name: Katase Nishihama beaches
ingredients.localName: 片瀬西浜・鵠沼海水浴場
ingredients.entityContext: Katase West Beach alongside Kugenuma forms part of the continuous Shonan beach setting used throughout the anime, with its wide sandy shore visible in background shots during episodes near Enoshima.

ingredients.name: Near the One Story Craftsman House
Honkugenuma Station
ingredients.entityContext: Honkugenuma Station on the Odakyu Enoshima Line appears in the anime as a local railway stop in the residential area near Sakuta's home, used in scenes depicting his daily commute in Fujisawa.

ingredients.name: Bic Camera Fujisawa store
ingredients.localName: ビックカメラ 藤沢店

ingredients.name: Enoshima Aquarium
ingredients.entityContext: The Shin-Enoshima Aquarium (Enosui) is a prominent location in the anime, visited by Sakuta and the characters during emotionally charged scenes, and its jellyfish tanks and ocean setting reinforce the coastal atmosphere central to the story.
